_README: PLACEHOLDER — REPLACE BEFORE Android RELEASE.
This file is a template. Until it's swapped for the real
google-services.json downloaded from your Firebase project,
Firebase.initializeApp() will throw on launch and AnalyticsService
silently no-ops on every Android device.
HOW TO REPLACE:
1. https://console.firebase.google.com -> Mirrorly project ->
Project settings -> Your apps -> tap the Android section ->
Add app (or 'Add another app' if Android is already there).
2. Package name: com.mirrorly.mirrorly  (MUST match exactly --
iOS uses com.mirrorly.app, Android is different).
3. App nickname: Mirrorly Android (or whatever).
4. Skip SHA-1 unless you wire Auth / Dynamic Links later.
5. Click 'Register app' -> 'Download google-services.json'.
6. Drop the downloaded file at android/app/google-services.json,
overwriting this placeholder.
The Android plugin (com.google.gms.google-services in
android/app/build.gradle.kts) is already wired and the
classpath in android/settings.gradle.kts is set -- once the
real file lands, Android analytics activates on next build.
